hyperedges.label: Dashboard Security: SQL Validation + PII Masking + Alert Fix Triangle
Chart IR Ecosystem: Grammar-of-Graphics → Vega-Lite IR → AskDB ChartEditor
Agent System Core Pillars: Adaptive Loop + SQLite Persistence + Dynamic Budget
Arrow Zero-Copy Data Pipeline
hyperedges.description: All four concepts collaborate in the Arrow zero-copy pipeline: DuckDB fetches via Arrow, the bridge converts between formats, PII masking runs on Arrow batches, and JSON serialization only occurs at the API boundary — eliminating Python list overhead throughout.

hyperedges.label: Agent Progress Visibility System
hyperedges.description: These five concepts together form the agent progress visibility system: phase-aware timeouts drive checklist updates, which are emitted as SSE steps, rendered by AnimatedChecklist, and post-execution accuracy is shown via the verification badge — all solving the 'dead spinner' problem.

hyperedges.label: User-Authored Chart Type Full Lifecycle
hyperedges.description: These five concepts define the complete lifecycle for user-authored chart types: compose a type via SpecTemplateComposer, browse via CustomTypePicker, execute high-code types in an iframe sandbox, package as .askdbviz, and share via the community gallery.

nodes.label: Chart System Redesign Sub-project D: Semantic Layer

properties.scope: Three-layer semantic intelligence system for NL understanding + visual identity + learning loop
three_layers: Layer 1: Linguistic Model (synonyms for tables/columns/values, grammatical phrasings, sample questions — AI bootstrapped from schema)
Layer 2: Persistent Color Map (per-connection Map<(column,value), hexColor> injected into Vega-Lite compiler)
Layer 3: Teach-by-Correction (detect chart edits, classify delta, surface non-blocking toast, write accepted corrections to model)
properties.advantage_over_competitors: Power BI and Looker require human-authored semantic metadata; AskDB bootstraps automatically from schema profiling + query history, human only reviews
phases: D1: Linguistic model + AI bootstrap + agent context injection
D2: Persistent color map + Vega-Lite compiler integration
D4: Settings UI + metric editor + Cmd-K + polish
properties.prompt_caching: All semantic context injected as single prompt-cached context block

properties.core_innovation: First BI tool showing cached answer instantly AND live verification transparently — no existing tool does this
properties.architecture: route_dual() -> cached_result SSE event -> normal agent loop -> live_correction SSE event
four_themes: Lazy Always-Correct (always fire live query even when cache fresh)
Write-Time Pre-Masking (PII mask at DuckDB write time)
critical_bugs: live_correction never fires when agent fails (Python truthiness conflation: `if x:` vs `if x is not None:`)
can_answer() not awaited in route_dual (coroutine object always truthy)
record_query_pattern never called — T4 warming dead code (subagent scope boundary gap)
AgentResult missing dual_response field (plan task with two intents, only one implemented)
asyncio.get_event_loop() deprecated in async context (use get_running_loop())
prevention_rules: Every async function call in sync-stepping code MUST go through _step_coro
Use `is not None` not truthiness for None-checking
Subagent cross-file wiring requires explicit 'Wire X in Y' task
Plan tasks with two intents must be split
asyncio.get_running_loop() inside async, get_event_loop() in sync
Every Pydantic Field numeric type needs ge/le bounds
After subagent DONE, grep for expected call sites

four_critical_gaps: PII masking bypass — memory and turbo tier results bypassed mask_dataframe()
No timing guards — slow tier blocked entire waterfall with no timeout
SQL injection in schema profiling — f-string interpolation with table names from DB
Cloud warehouse blocking — COUNT(*) on TB-scale Snowflake/BigQuery blocks 30-120s
properties.architectural_decision: Template Method ABC pattern (BaseTier.answer() non-overridable, calls abstract _answer() then _apply_masking()) — 1/20 council votes but structurally un-bypassable vs convention-based (10/20 votes)
lessons: replace_all edits may miss copies with different surrounding context — always grep for old pattern after
Exception handlers on security-critical functions must return SAFE DEFAULT (TierResult(hit=False, data={})), never original input
Constructing new object from existing: explicitly copy ALL mutable fields (dict/list by reference)
Test the PRODUCTION code path not just convenient path (FastAPI sync path vs async test path)
Minority council votes can be architecturally correct — evaluate on structural merit not vote count

nodes.label: Plan: Redis Integration + SSE Live Tile Updates

systems_wired: query result cache (Redis SETEX + in-memory fallback)
rate limiter (Redis sorted sets + in-memory fallback)
SSE tile subscription (Redis pub/sub channel qc:tile_updates:{dashboard_id})
properties.design_principle: get_redis() returns None if unavailable — all callers must have in-memory fallback

properties.approach: 4-phase incremental build: Schema Intelligence -> Query Memory -> DuckDB Turbo -> Decomposition
invariants: Invariant-1: Read-only DB enforcement never weakened
Invariant-2: PII masking via mask_dataframe() before data reaches users or LLM
Invariant-3: Two-step query flow not collapsed
Invariant-5: ChromaDB namespace isolation per connection
Invariant-6: Atomic file writes pattern preserved
Invariant-7: Existing SSE event types backward-compatible (additive only)
failure_modes: waterfall tangled if/else (mitigated by strategy pattern)
ChromaDB collection pollution (separate collection for query memory)
new tiers add latency to happy path
unvalidated_assumptions: schema metadata available for SAP HANA/IBM DB2/Databricks
ChromaDB hit rate >=85% for repeated patterns

properties.purpose: Table-style editor for persistent color assignments — column + value + hex color per row
Non-blocking toast stack for teach-by-correction suggestions
properties.behavior: Shows one suggestion at a time from correctionSuggestions[] in store. Auto-dismisses after 8s with animated progress bar. Accept writes to linguistic model or color map via D0 API.

accepted_actions.synonym: Appends newField to synonyms.columns[oldField][] via api.saveLinguisticModel()
accepted_actions.color_map: Adds domain/range pairs to color map via api.saveColorMap()
accepted_actions.measure_default: Deferred to D4, just dismisses for now

properties.ui: bottom-right absolute, 360px wide, glassmorphism, progress bar animation

properties.purpose: Pure function diff of two ChartSpecs -> CorrectionSuggestion[]
detectable_corrections: synonym: field renamed in encoding channel (bField.field !== aField.field)
measure_default: aggregation changed (same field, different aggregate)

properties.purpose: 5-tab settings page for managing the semantic layer
Pure function: given (spec, resultProfile, gpuTier, frameBudgetState, instancePressure) -> RenderStrategy
properties.why_pure: Easy to unit test (no DOM), easy to log in telemetry, easy for agent to query current tier

properties.purpose: Track ALL renderer instances (Vega SVG/Canvas, MapLibre, deck.gl, Three.js) with memory-aware eviction, not just WebGL context count
properties.eviction: LRU + prefer evicting deck over vega-canvas when both LRU (frees WebGL context)

properties.why: Single IR enables: agent emits ChartSpec, user edits via Marks card, renderer compiles to target engine, JSON-Patch undo/redo, voice drives same spec — all identical substrate

properties.rationale: Replaces flat {tiles[], layout[]} with hierarchical {tabs[{sections[{tiles[], layout[]}]}]}
properties.migration: Existing dashboards get 1 default tab 'Overview' + 1 default section 'General' — zero breaking changes

properties.mechanism: Hybrid: frontend instant filter + backend accurate re-query
properties.flow: Click bar in Tile A -> setCrossFilter({field, value}) -> all tiles filter rows locally (<50ms) -> background refreshBulk with WHERE clause -> replace when backend responds
properties.clear: Clear Filter badge at top showing field + value

properties.not_captured: tile data (too large, re-fetched on load)
properties.backend: Bookmark CRUD endpoints + bookmarks in allowed keys for update_dashboard()

properties.principle: All tier results flow through Arrow bridge. JSON serialization happens ONLY at API response layer (agent_routes.py, query_routes.py), never inside tiers.
properties.pii_extension: mask_record_batch(batch) in pii_masking.py — Arrow-native masking with pandas fallback for edge cases

properties.context: Evolution of DuckDB twin in global-comp branch
properties.layer1: Full Local Tables — tables below 50K rows copied entirely (fixes broken JOINs on lookup tables like countries/categories/status codes)
properties.layer2: Query-Pattern-Aware Sampling — bias sample toward recent dates, increase sample size for high-frequency tables based on .data/query_patterns/{conn_id}.json
properties.layer3: Materialized Aggregates — pre-compute common GROUP BY rollups (daily/weekly/monthly) during twin sync

properties.innovation: First BI tool showing cached answer instantly AND live verification transparently
sse_events: cached_result (with staleness badge: green Fresh / amber Cached / red Stale)
live_correction (green Verified or amber Updated with diff)
properties.always_correct_flag: DUAL_RESPONSE_ALWAYS_CORRECT — fire live query even when cache is fresh
properties.critical_fix: live_correction guard must be `if _dual_cached_content is not None:` not `if ... and self._result.final_answer:` — empty string is falsy in Python

properties.pattern: Template Method ABC: BaseTier.answer() is concrete and non-overridable. It calls abstract _answer() then _apply_masking(). __init_subclass__ raises TypeError if subclass tries to override answer().
properties.why: Convention-based security erodes with team changes; structural enforcement persists
properties.council_vote: 1/20 votes (minority) chosen over 10/20 (router boundary) because structurally un-bypassable
properties.masking_failure_rule: On exception in _apply_masking, return TierResult(hit=False, data={}) — NEVER return original unmasked data
properties.idempotent: mask_dataframe() is idempotent — masked values don't match PII regexes on re-pass

tiers: Tier 3: OpenAI Realtime (user BYOK key, ~$3.60/hr, 200-300ms, voice OUT, interruption detection)
properties.security_pattern: Ephemeral token mint: backend reads user Fernet-encrypted key, calls vendor mint-ephemeral endpoint, returns short-lived token (60s TTL) to frontend. Browser opens WebSocket directly to vendor — permanent key NEVER ships to browser.
properties.whisper_model: Whisper Tiny (~75MB), cached in IndexedDB after user permission
properties.wake_word: Hey Vega (openWakeWord MIT, single .onnx model ~150KB shipped with frontend)

properties.role: Replaces query_decomposer.py for query optimization inside LiveTier. Does NOT replace waterfall routing.
properties.key_capability: Federated pushdown: pushes heavy aggregations to remote DB, lightweight filtering to local twin
properties.fallback: DATAFUSION_FALLBACK_TO_DECOMPOSER flag — falls back to query_decomposer.py on DataFusion plan failure
properties.agent_visibility: DataFusion planning emitted as agent step with strategy, estimated vs actual timing

nodes.label: ML Engine: AutoML Pipeline with Celery

properties.data_flow: DuckDB Twin -> fetch_arrow_table() -> pl.from_arrow() [zero-copy] -> Polars feature engineering -> .to_numpy() -> sklearn/xgboost training -> results -> Polars -> Arrow -> JSON
ingest_modes: Full Dataset (full live source, bypasses twin)
properties.security: ML_AUTO_EXCLUDE_PII=True drops PII-flagged columns before training; model files stored 0o600

properties.description: 17-task plan to add ModelProvider ABC, AnthropicProvider adapter, per-user Fernet-encrypted API key storage/validation, full QueryCopilot→DataLens rename, 5-step interactive onboarding, Account page key management

All 4 Themes combined: Lazy Always-Correct dual-stream (T1), Staleness-Gated conditional (T2), Write-Time Pre-Masking (T3), Behavior-Driven Warming (T4); progressive rollout behind independent feature flags
Adaptive loop + SQLite session persistence + structured progress + dynamic tool budget + lightweight planning + continue/resume + Claude Code-style session history; 14/14 tasks pass
Dim/measure classification + tile SQL regen + { autocomplete formula editor + function-aware autocomplete + validation gate + chat-bubble agent panel + quick-action buttons + smart trending badge

Resolution plan for all remaining adversarial findings: Sprint1 immediate fixes, Sprint2 pre-launch security, Sprint3 pre-monetization, Sprint4 pre-scale, Sprint5 multi-tenant; 24 items ~20-25hrs total

Milestone-driven resolution of all P2/P3 adversarial findings: OTP HMAC storage, PBKDF2 KDF upgrade, dependency pinning, Windows file permissions, in-memory OTP rate limiter, collected_steps cap, QueryMemory singleton, refresh_twin atomic swap, PII word-boundary regex, health endpoint degraded status, is_verified lock guard

Manual regression checklist for BI editability release: Slice1 field classification + schema columns + SQL regen, Slice2 formula autocomplete + validation gate + CRUD, Slice3 trending badge + agent chat bubbles + quick actions + input styling, general dashboard regression + build verification

Full dashboard overhaul spec: fix broken buttons/KPI toolbar/comment badge/notes deletion, parallel refresh, auto-save layout, PresentationEngine with bin-packing, conversational editing, anomaly narrator, NL alerts, screenshot-to-dashboard, drill-down, what-if sliders, shareable links, CSV export, embeddable widget, version history; 8 critical ADV-FIX changes incorporated

properties.guardrails: 6 max tool calls, 30s timeout, 3 SQL retries
properties.description: Original agent spec: Claude Tool Use API, 6 tools, session memory with auto-compaction at ~8K tokens, dockable/draggable agent panel for dashboard, SSE streaming, auto-execute toggle, big data strategy (ChromaDB-first schema, LIMIT 5000, all computation pushed to DB)

21-feature predictive intelligence layer: behavior tracking → compaction → purge → prediction; 3 suggestions after every response; session tracking + opt-in consent + adaptive complexity + time-aware patterns + anomaly alerts + analyst persona profiles; hybrid rules+Claude; feature flags per feature in config.py

properties.council_decision: Combined Theme2+3 — phased waterfall with anonymized SQL intents
properties.description: Three-tier waterfall optimization: schema intelligence cache (~7ms), ChromaDB collective memory (<100ms), DuckDB local twin (<100ms, opt-in Turbo Mode), optimized live + decomposition (streaming); anonymized SQL intents for shared memory; schema staleness/drift is top unanimous risk

Provider abstraction (ModelProvider ABC + AnthropicProvider with circuit breaker), per-user Fernet-encrypted API key, demo user gets platform key, brand renamed DataLens with brandPurple token, 5-step onboarding wizard, BYOK model selection

properties.council_decision: All 4 Themes Combined with progressive rollout
properties.description: DuckDB turbo + Progressive Dual-Response UX: cached_result SSE within 300ms, live_correction SSE within 5s; staleness-gated TTL; write-time PII pre-masking; behavior-driven cache warming; Phase2 materialized slice cache for enterprise

Adaptive loop: heuristic initial budget (dashboard=20, complex=15, simple=8), auto-extend in +10 increments to 100; SQLite WAL .data/agent_sessions.db, 50 sessions/user cap; plan step as checklist; /continue endpoint; dialect hints BigQuery/Snowflake/MySQL/MSSQL; context compaction every 6 tool calls

Tableau-class dashboard density: tighter typography (kpiValue 22px, tileTitle 12px), 6 new tile types, dark+light dual theme, agent dashboard generation with Blueprint-Then-Approve workflow, connect-time preflight stats (~20 aggregate queries), animated time-series, conversational drill-in

Full DataLens→AskDB rebrand across all files; elastic per-phase timeout architecture replacing single wall-clock; Claude Code-style progress checklist with phase badges + elapsed + ETA; cancel button wired to SSE close + backend cancel endpoint; per-user concurrency cap of 2

Dim/measure auto-detect (numeric=measure, string/date/bool=dimension) with user override; TileEditor shows all DB schema columns; SQL regen on column swap (complex→agent-assist); { autocomplete with SUM→measures-first, COUNT→dimensions-first; trending badge only on temporal X-axis; chat bubbles with right/left alignment

Two rounds 20-analyst adversarial testing; P0: DuckDB tuple unpack, thread pool sleep→Event, JWT algorithm allowlist; P1: admin JWT collapse, waterfall bypass query limits, Unicode PII bypass, /continue concurrency guard, non-atomic auth writes; Round2 P1: health endpoint ID leak, ChromaDB proliferation, word-boundary SQL masking

properties.root_cause: restore_dashboard_version held _lock then called save_dashboard_version which also takes _lock — non-reentrant threading.Lock deadlock on same thread; async def amplified to freeze entire uvicorn worker
properties.fix: Extract _save_version_no_lock helper; save_dashboard_version is thin wrapper; restore calls no-lock helper directly; matches _auto_version_snapshot convention already in file

properties.decision: Create ModelProvider ABC with complete(), complete_stream(), complete_with_tools(), validate_key() methods; ContentBlock and ProviderToolResponse dataclasses abstract Anthropic-specific types; anthropic_provider.py is the ONLY file that imports anthropic SDK
properties.rationale: Isolates LLM provider from 14 call sites; enables BYOK (user supplies own Anthropic API key Fernet-encrypted in profile); enables future non-Anthropic providers; thin 1:1 wrapper preserves behavior

properties.decision: Use SQLite with WAL mode at .data/agent_sessions.db for agent session persistence; threading.Lock for write safety; 50 sessions/user cap with auto-purge; replaces in-memory dict (backend) and localStorage (frontend)
properties.rationale: WAL mode supports concurrent reads; busy_timeout=5000 handles write contention; email-scoped queries enforce Invariant-3 (session isolation); SQLite bundled in Python stdlib, no extra infra

properties.decision: Heuristic initial budget (dashboard=20, complex=15, simple=8) with auto-extension in +10 increments to safety cap of 100; extensions logged to audit trail; no user confirmation needed for extension
properties.rationale: Previous 12-call hard cap interrupted dashboard creation with 5+ tiles; quality first means never interrupting user work mid-build; 100-call safety cap prevents runaway cost

properties.decision: Show cached answer (cached_result SSE) within 300ms, then fire live query and yield live_correction SSE; cached answers show freshness badge (age, color-coded: green/amber/red); live correction shows Confirmed or Updated with diff
properties.rationale: No BI tool does this; Tableau shows stale extract OR slow live; this shows both simultaneously with transparency; builds trust via freshness badges; all features behind independent flags so any layer can be disabled

properties.decision: Demo user (demo@askdb.dev) gets platform Anthropic API key from settings.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY via provider_registry.py; all other users must supply own key (Fernet-encrypted in per-user profile); anthropic_provider.py is the sole import site for the anthropic SDK
properties.rationale: Zero hosting cost for non-demo users; BYOD model; provider_registry.get_provider_for_user() is single lookup point; encrypting user keys with same Fernet as DB passwords reuses existing infrastructure

properties.decision: Auto-detect from SQL column types: numeric→measure, string/date/boolean→dimension; user can toggle any field; classification stored per-tile as fieldClassifications dict; custom metrics always classified as measure (non-toggleable); fieldClassification.js utility module
properties.rationale: Prevents station (string) appearing in measure dropdowns; enables function-aware formula autocomplete (SUM→measures-first, COUNT→dimensions-first); three chart creation paths: SQL Editor, Agent, manual picker

properties.decision: Extract _save_version_no_lock() as private helper; public save_dashboard_version is thin with _lock: wrapper; restore_dashboard_version calls no-lock helper directly; reject RLock alternative (masks bug, slower, breaks convention)
properties.rationale: threading.Lock is non-reentrant; nested acquisition on same thread deadlocks forever; async def over sync I/O means single deadlock freezes entire uvicorn worker and all subsequent requests; _auto_version_snapshot already established this pattern in same file

properties.decision: Replace \b word-boundary in SQL intent masking with (?<![a-zA-Z]) / (?![a-zA-Z]) lookarounds; catches employee_ssn and user_salary but NOT business (sin surrounded by alpha chars); precompile regex over 30 patterns
properties.rationale: \b treats underscore as word character so \bssn\b does not match employee_ssn; lookarounds that treat _ as transparent correctly catch compound column names; over-masking is safer than under-masking

properties.decision: Replace single 120s WALL_CLOCK_LIMIT with per-phase budgets: planning 30s, schema discovery 60s, SQL generation 30s, DB execution 300s (configurable per-connection), verification 30s, session hard cap 1800s; four independent clocks made aware of each other
properties.rationale: Single wall clock killed complex queries at 120s regardless of phase; cloud warehouses need up to 300s for query execution; planning should never be slow; 1800s hard cap prevents forgotten sessions

properties.rule: All three layers must agree; no task may weaken any layer; applies to DuckDB twin queries as well

mask_dataframe() must run before any data reaches users or the LLM; BaseTier template method _apply_masking() enforces this on every tier result; cached results must be PII-masked before display even if live correction fails
properties.cached_safety: Invariant-9: Progressive Dual-Response must never show unmasked cached data even if live correction fails

properties.rule: /generate then /execute; never collapse; conversational editing of tiles cannot patch sql/rows/columns fields; only allowed patch fields: chartType, title, subtitle, palette, activeMeasures, selectedMeasure, visualConfig
Write to {path}.tmp, flush, then os.replace(tmp, path); applies to user data, API key storage, DuckDB twin refresh, OAuth state, verifications; new storage operations must follow this pattern

New SSE event types (tier_routing, progress, tier_hit, cached_result, live_correction, plan, budget_extension) are additive only; never remove or rename existing types; frontend ignores unknown types gracefully via fallback renderer

FERNET_SECRET_KEY (if set) takes priority, else PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 (480K iterations, static salt from app identity) of JWT_SECRET_KEY; changing either key invalidates all saved DB passwords AND API keys; encrypt_password/decrypt_password are the only interfaces
properties.upgrade: Upgraded from bare SHA-256 in Sprint A3 hardening

properties.rule: Session memory never shared across users; all SQLite session queries include WHERE email = ?; load_session(chat_id, wrong_email) returns None; session store enforces isolation

properties.fix: OTP codes stored as hmac.new(JWT_SECRET_KEY, code, sha256).hexdigest() instead of plaintext in pending_verifications.json; verification uses hmac.compare_digest() for timing-safe comparison

nodes.label: Fernet KDF Upgrade to PBKDF2 (Sprint A3)

properties.fix: Replace hashlib.sha256(key).digest() with PBKDF2HMAC(SHA256, 480K iterations, static salt); deterministic same key→same Fernet key so existing passwords still decrypt
properties.rationale: PBKDF2 480K iterations adds ~200ms compute per derivation attempt, raising offline brute-force cost by ~5 orders of magnitude vs bare SHA-256; static salt acceptable because JWT_SECRET_KEY is high-entropy (64+ chars)

properties.fix: _SAFE_JWT_ALGORITHMS = {HS256, HS384, HS512} enforced at config.py startup; rejects none algorithm (CVE-2015-9235)
unicodedata.normalize(NFKC, col) in pii_masking.py before pattern matching; prevents fullwidth Unicode bypass (ｅｍａｉｌ not recognized as email without normalization)
Replace file-based rate limiting (deletable log file) with collections.defaultdict(list) keyed by identifier with TTL eviction; threading.Lock for thread safety

properties.description: ModelProvider ABC with complete(), complete_stream(), complete_with_tools(), validate_key(), supports_prompt_caching(), supports_vision(); ProviderResponse, ContentBlock, ProviderToolResponse dataclasses; InvalidKeyError exception

AnthropicProvider(ModelProvider) wrapping Anthropic SDK; circuit breaker (5 failures → 30s cooldown with jitter, per-API-key isolation); prompt caching support; native tool-use translation to ContentBlock; validate_key() sends minimal 1-token message; ONLY file that imports anthropic

get_provider_for_user(email) resolves correct provider + API key; demo user (demo@askdb.dev) gets platform key; other users get Fernet-decrypted key from profile; ANTHROPIC_MODELS catalog; get_fallback_model() helper

AgentSessionStore singleton; SQLite WAL at .data/agent_sessions.db; save_session(), load_session(), list_sessions(), delete_session(), purge_oldest(); threading.Lock for writes; email-scoped all queries; 50 sessions/user cap

classifyColumns() auto-detects dim/measure from data types; isDateColumn() heuristic (>80% of first 20 rows parse as dates); MEASURE_FUNCTIONS config; getFieldSuggestions() for function-aware autocomplete context

Controlled textarea with { autocomplete dropdown; detects enclosing function name (detectEnclosingFunction); sections: Dimensions / Measures; SUM→measures-first, COUNT→dimensions-first; FM-3 mitigation: only triggers on { keypress not paste/programmatic

route_dual() added: returns (cached_result, live_callable) tuple; uses _step_async pattern same as _route_sync_impl; if DUAL_RESPONSE_ALWAYS_CORRECT always returns run_live; staleness-gated (T2): if cache_age < TTL and not ALWAYS_CORRECT, skip live; TierResult extended with cache_age_seconds and is_stale fields

Write-time masking (T3): if WRITE_TIME_MASKING, call mask_dataframe() before writing to DuckDB; adds masked_at_write column to metadata; BaseTier skips read-time masking for turbo tier if masked_at_write=True; get_warm_priorities() returns top-10 tables by access frequency for behavior warming (T4); refresh_twin atomic swap (.refresh.tmp.duckdb → os.replace)

Migrated to ModelProvider interface (no direct anthropic import); dual-response integration with try/except guard; AgentStep extended with cache_age_seconds/is_correction/diff_summary; dynamic tool budget 8/15/20→100; _progress tracker {goal, completed, pending, total_tool_calls}; _generate_plan() Sonnet call for complex/dashboard; DIALECT_HINTS BigQuery/Snowflake/MySQL; _compact_tool_context() every 6 calls; threading.Event replaces time.sleep polling; phase-aware timeouts
query_twin() returned 3-tuple from validate() but code unpacked as 2-tuple; all turbo SQL validation was broken; sibling bug in dashboard_routes also fixed

nodes.label: P0: Thread Pool Exhaustion via time.sleep() Polling

properties.description: ask_user pause used time.sleep(0.3) polling loop holding one thread per parked session; thread pool exhaustion at low concurrent user count
properties.fix: threading.Event.wait() replaces polling; /respond and /cancel signal event; 16x reduction in idle wakeups

nodes.label: P1: Waterfall Early-Return Bypasses Query Limits

properties.description: All 3 waterfall early-return paths (schema/memory/turbo tier hits) did not call increment_query_stats(); daily query limits not enforced for cache hits
properties.fix: All 3 early-return paths now call increment_query_stats()

properties.description: /continue endpoint missing _active_agents increment/decrement and no _running check; allowed >2 concurrent agent sessions per user
properties.fix: Full concurrency guard + duplicate loop prevention added

nodes.label: P1 Round2: Health Endpoint Connection ID Leak

properties.description: Health endpoint returned per-connection breakdown leaking connection IDs; 4/20 analysts flagged
properties.fix: Removed per-conn breakdown; returns aggregate counts only (healthy_connections, unhealthy_connections)

properties.description: Cleanup scheduler created new QueryMemory() (new PersistentClient) every 6 hours in loop
properties.fix: QueryMemory() instantiated once before while True loop; CLAUDE.md constraint: module-level singleton, never create per-request

properties.symptom: Dashboard freezing on restore; page never loads after refresh
properties.root_cause: restore_dashboard_version held _lock then called save_dashboard_version which takes same non-reentrant _lock; deadlock on same thread; async def amplified to freeze entire uvicorn worker
properties.fix: _save_version_no_lock helper extracted; restore calls no-lock helper; save_dashboard_version is thin wrapper

properties.rule: anthropic_provider.py is the ONLY file that should import anthropic; all other modules use ModelProvider ABC via provider_registry.get_provider_for_user(); BYOK verification: grep -rn import anthropic backend/ | grep -v anthropic_provider.py expects 0 results
.chroma/querycopilot path NOT renamed during DataLens/AskDB rebrand; filesystem path change breaks existing ChromaDB data; only user-facing strings are renamed

nodes.label: Constraint: Tile SQL Never Patchable via NL Edit

properties.rule: Conversational tile editing allowlist: {chartType, title, subtitle, palette, activeMeasures, selectedMeasure, visualConfig}; sql, columns, rows, blendConfig, dataSources are NEVER patchable via Claude JSON patch; SQL changes must route through /generate + /execute two-step flow

user_storage.py uses non-reentrant threading.Lock by convention; never switch to RLock (masks nested-lock bugs, slower on hot paths, breaks convention); instead extract no-lock helpers for code that must call locking functions from within a lock

nodes.label: Constraint: Version Snapshots on Explicit Actions Only

properties.rule: Dashboard version snapshots must NOT fire on auto-save or auto-refresh; only on explicit user actions (tile add/remove, section changes, manual save); rate-limit to max 1 snapshot per 60 seconds; parameter snapshot=False on updateDashboard for auto-save calls

nodes.label: Constraint: Share Tokens Must Be Opaque Server-Side

properties.rule: Shared dashboard links must use opaque random UUID token stored server-side mapping to {dashboard_id, exp, created_by}; NOT JWT in URL (leaks to Referer headers); token is a lookup key, not a secret

properties.routing: ProtectedRoute enforces onboarding; login truth table: tutorial_completed=false→/onboarding, true+has-key→/dashboard, true+no-key→/onboarding?step=3
properties.description: Interactive onboarding replacing Tutorial page; guides new users through BYOK key entry and first connection

Enabling a schema column not in current tile data triggers backend /dashboards/generate-column-sql endpoint; uses sqlglot to rewrite SELECT list; complex SQL (CTE, >2 JOINs) returns error complex_sql → frontend shows use Agent prompt; simple tiles get auto-regen; all output validated through sql_validator

Trending badge only displays when X-axis column is detected as date/time type via isDateColumn() heuristic; suppressed on categorical X-axis (fixes bug: ascending-sorted categorical data was showing badge); linear regression algorithm unchanged

POST /api/v1/agent/continue {chat_id}: loads session from SQLite, extracts progress_json, injects as <progress> block in system prompt, creates AgentEngine with synthetic question Continue the previous task, streams SSE same as /run, saves updated session on completion

On connect, run ~20 lightweight aggregate queries (COUNT/AVG/MIN/MAX/GROUP BY returning <500KB total); Sonnet generates blueprint proposal of 8-12 tiles; agent presents via ask_user; user selects tiles with 1-click; then agent builds; soft approval gate preserves two-step invariant; stats stored in schema_cache/{conn_id}_stats.json

query_memory.record_query_pattern() tracks per-table access frequency in .data/query_patterns/{conn_id}.json; duckdb_twin.get_warm_priorities() returns top-10 tables by frequency; auto-warm on connect prioritizes frequently-queried tables; BEHAVIOR_WARMING_ENABLED=False default

properties.note: D3 depends on D2 color map
Editing freedom spec extends the base redesign
Performance spec solves sequential tile refresh and bundle bloat discovered during dashboard build
Redis + SSE live tiles implements the dashboard real-time refresh requirements
Fix spec addresses bugs introduced and identified during editing freedom build
Adversarial fixes address security/reliability bugs from the fix spec Phase 1
Global comp is the broader product vision; chart sub-projects are implementation epics within it
B is gated on A reaching Phase 0-1 (IR + router must exist)
C builds on A's UserChartTypeRegistry foundation
D builds on A's SemanticFieldRail and chart-ir/semantic foundation
Voice infrastructure detailed in Sub-project A spec section 5
Agent system plan established SSE infrastructure that dual-response leveraged
Plan built the system; hardening postmortem documents the adversarial fixing
ColorMapEditor reused in SemanticSettings Color Map tab
correctionDetector produces CorrectionSuggestion[] that CorrectionToast renders

hyperedges.label: Dashboard Security: SQL Validation + PII Masking + Alert Fix Triangle
Chart IR Ecosystem: Grammar-of-Graphics → Vega-Lite IR → AskDB ChartEditor
Agent System Core Pillars: Adaptive Loop + SQLite Persistence + Dynamic Budget
Arrow Zero-Copy Data Pipeline
hyperedges.description: All four concepts collaborate in the Arrow zero-copy pipeline: DuckDB fetches via Arrow, the bridge converts between formats, PII masking runs on Arrow batches, and JSON serialization only occurs at the API boundary — eliminating Python list overhead throughout.

hyperedges.label: Agent Progress Visibility System
hyperedges.description: These five concepts together form the agent progress visibility system: phase-aware timeouts drive checklist updates, which are emitted as SSE steps, rendered by AnimatedChecklist, and post-execution accuracy is shown via the verification badge — all solving the 'dead spinner' problem.

hyperedges.label: User-Authored Chart Type Full Lifecycle
hyperedges.description: These five concepts define the complete lifecycle for user-authored chart types: compose a type via SpecTemplateComposer, browse via CustomTypePicker, execute high-code types in an iframe sandbox, package as .askdbviz, and share via the community gallery.
